name: Journey Loop Hardening
purpose: Make the Guided AI Journey -> M365 -> Journey receipt loop dedupe-friendly, readable by portalEventId, and recoverable when Journey shows crm_failed_or_timed_out.

currentState.httpIntakeFlowDisplayName: GAIL - Custom site intake to CRM (create-only, HTTP)
currentState.currentReceiptCallback: M365 sends signed ack after CRM item creation when server-side ack endpoint and secret are configured.

sharePointFields.purpose: Primary Journey event dedupe and read-back key.
Secondary correlation key for Journey/M365 replay and operator support.

deferredFields.reason: Derive receipt state from the Journey ledger for now. Add CRM-local status only if operators need to work receipts from CRM directly.

recommendedDefault.duplicatePolicy: If one existing CRM item matches the same PortalEventId, do not create a new CRM item. Return the existing CRM item id/url to Journey with crmStatus=existing. If more than one item matches, stop and require Adam review.
recommendedDefault.replayPolicy: Journey operator retry/replay must reuse the same portalEventId. A replay should either create the missing CRM item or acknowledge the existing one; it must not silently create duplicate work.
recommendedDefault.cleanupPolicy: No delete by default. Leave historical B7 proof rows as evidence. Backfill first-class fields only after exact item ids and live write scope are approved.

authority.schemaChange: G2/R2 or G3/R3 depending on exact tenant surface
authority.flowUpdate: G3/R3 because it changes an active HTTP ingress and Journey callback path
authority.replayTest: G3/R3 if it creates CRM items or calls Journey

scope: Add PortalEventId and SourceCorrelationId fields to CRM - New Signals.
Update the existing HTTP intake flow to populate the approved first-class fields.
Add pre-create idempotency lookup by PortalEventId.
Send Journey receipt ack for both created and existing CRM item outcomes.
Run one no-real-client replay proof using a synthetic/internal Journey event.
stopConditions: No callback URL accepted from payload.
No browser-side intake or ack secret.

acceptance: B8 local packet names exact fields, duplicate policy, replay policy, cleanup policy, evidence paths, stop conditions, and approval phrase.
After live approval and implementation, a Journey event can be found from PortalEventId without scraping SourceText.
Replaying the same PortalEventId does not create an unreviewed duplicate CRM item.
Journey receives a receipt ack for both new-create and existing-item replay outcomes.
Older synthetic evidence rows are left alone or backfilled only under an exact approval scope.
